raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "prescriptive instructional guidance on therapeutic procedure",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "suggestion-based intervention requires periodic renewal whose frequency must be reduced over time",
    "measurable progress toward a definitive endpoint ('cure') exists and can guide dosing",
    "the same tapering schedule is necessary in every case",
    "once suggestions cease, the desired state remains permanently achieved"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a procedural algorithm; logical flow from frequent renewal to spaced withdrawal to termination is clear under its own premises",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the underlying model of suggestion therapy must be empirically valid, progress must be reliably observable, and a complete, stable cure must be attainable by this schedule",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the intervention lacks reliable curative power, 'progress' is illusory or subjective, or permanent change does not result from spaced repetition"
}
